commands.review_summary:
The PR makes gateway provider-auth prewarm shutdown clear the provider-auth state owner and adds delayed-start and active-worker cancellation coverage.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +77. Total +81 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can track an active provider-auth warm worker while the gateway sidecar stop omits the state owner’s cancellation path. No real current-main gateway-process reproduction was supplied.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No concrete code defect remains for an automated repair; the contributor must supply real gateway behavior proof before normal maintainer merge review.

target.title: fix(gateway): clear provider auth prewarm on stop
target.body:
## Summary

- Clear provider-auth prewarm state from the full-gateway prewarm sidecar's `stop()` path.
- Cancel the active prewarm through the existing provider-auth state owner instead of relying on process shutdown.
- Preserve the actual local-agent issue as follow-up by removing the unsupported CLI-wide cleanup and closing claim.
- Add focused gateway and provider-auth lifecycle coverage, and keep CLI exit coverage aligned with the CLI resources it actually owns.

Related to #92464

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Full-gateway provider-auth prewarm can start an off-main-thread provider-auth warm worker. When the gateway sidecar is stopped before or during that prewarm, the sidecar should clear provider-auth state so the tracked warm worker is cancelled by its lifecycle owner instead of being left to process shutdown.

**Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `6c4e6e93b6b88849aa268095dd43fc8356330665`. No provider credentials, network calls, or external model calls were used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
git diff --check
```

**Evidence after fix:** Focused lifecycle tests prove the gateway sidecar clears provider-auth state when stopped before a delayed prewarm fires and while a prewarm is already active. The provider-auth worker test uses a real worker-thread script that would write a marker after its delay; after `clearCurrentProviderAuthState()` runs, the warm promise settles and the marker is absent, proving the active worker was terminated.

```text
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001029/openclaw-92464

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  102 passed (102)
   Start at  03:04:31
   Duration  14.52s (transform 4.36s, setup 733ms, import 411ms, tests 12.71s, environment 0ms)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001029/openclaw-92464

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  91 passed (91)
   Start at  03:04:51
   Duration  2.09s (transform 1.01s, setup 539ms, import 570ms, tests 658ms, environment 0ms)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001029/openclaw-92464

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  19 passed (19)
   Start at  03:04:59
   Duration  5.29s (transform 2.09s, setup 437ms, import 3.95s, tests 639ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 59.12s
```

Additional static checks:

```text
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
# no output

git diff --check
# no output
```

**Observed result after fix:** Stopping the gateway provider-auth prewarm sidecar clears provider-auth state in both covered stop timings: before the delayed prewarm starts and after an active prewarm has begun. In the active-prewarm case, the cancellation predicate flips to true and the tracked worker is terminated before it can complete its delayed marker write. CLI teardown coverage remains green after removing the unsupported CLI-wide provider-auth cleanup.

**What was not tested:** This PR no longer claims to fix the scale-sensitive `openclaw agent --local --json` retained-resource report from #92464. The exact Docker image, 10,000-entry local memory workload, external OpenAI provider credentials, and before/after active-handle comparison for the local-agent hang were not tested here. Those need a separate root-cause fix for the local-agent memory/embedding lifecycle before the issue can be closed.
target.author: mushuiyu886
